vol.label: Mixed — VIX calming, but enabling-layer names still bleeding
decomp: VIX -1.98% to 15.82 — second straight down day, broad fear receding
KOSPI +5.76%, SK Hynix +10.88%, Samsung +8.22% — a sharp Korea rebound
But DRAM -7.94%, GLW -10.81%, LITE -9.09%, AMKR -12.93%, POWI -9.07% — enabling-layer names still falling despite the Korea bounce
EWY -2.89% to $180.14 — lagging the KOSPI rally, still well below its 197.06 SMA20
Gold still +1.26%, holding most of its safe-haven gains

bias: A genuinely confusing session. Korea itself staged a sharp rebound — KOSPI +5.76% to 8,088.35, SK Hynix +10.88%, Samsung +8.22% — closing the gap to the 8,200 gate threshold to just over 1%, and USDKRW eased to 1,530.15, effectively sitting right on the 1,530 line. That's the most encouraging Korea print of the entire episode. But it didn't carry through to the US-listed enabling-layer names, which kept falling: DRAM -7.94%, GLW -10.81%, LITE -9.09%, AMKR -12.93%, POWI -9.07%, CRDO -6.63%, MU -5.49% — a continuation of Thursday's US-session rout even as the Korean source of the stress improved. EWY itself only partially recovered (-2.89% on the day, still well below its 197.06 SMA20), lagging the KOSPI move. Meanwhile VIX eased for a second straight session (-1.98% to 15.82) and financials stabilized (CACC +0.21%, COF +0.15%, only ALLY -1.65%) — so broad risk sentiment looks calmer even as individual chip-adjacent names keep bleeding. Separately, the first new H.4.1 print in over a week landed net liquidity-positive: TGA fell to $880.2B (a $38.5B drawdown) while Reserves rose to $2.967T, comfortably outweighing the $1.17B rise in RRP. Korea gate still 0/3, but two of three conditions (KOSPI, USDKRW) are now within a hair of flipping — this is the closest the gate has been to opening all episode. Read with caution given today is a low-volume Friday-into-holiday session.

counterargs.thesis: Korea itself is healing faster than the US-listed proxies that were supposed to track it
supporting: KOSPI +5.76%, SK Hynix +10.88%, Samsung +8.22% — a sharp, broad Korean rebound
USDKRW eased to 1,530.15, right at the gate threshold — closest approach all episode
VIX down a second straight session (-1.98%) — broad risk sentiment calming
First H.4.1 print in over a week is net liquidity-positive (TGA drawdown outweighs RRP rise)
against: EWY only -2.89% recovery, still well below its 197.06 SMA20 — the ETF itself hasn't confirmed the KOSPI move
DRAM -7.94%, GLW -10.81%, LITE -9.09%, AMKR -12.93%, POWI -9.07% — enabling-layer names kept falling despite the Korea bounce
Low-volume, pre-holiday Friday session — moves may not hold once full liquidity returns Monday
Korea gate still 0/3 — nothing here is confirmed until KOSPI actually clears 8,200 and USDKRW closes below 1,530
counterargs.read: The relationship between Korea's own market and the US-listed proxies that are supposed to track it has now inverted twice in two days — first EWY flat while KOSPI crashed, now KOSPI roaring back while US names keep falling. That's a sign the US-listed basket may be trading on its own momentum/rebalancing flows rather than tracking the underlying Korean fundamentals in real time. Worth revisiting Monday with a full session of liquidity before drawing conclusions either way.

trade_table.rationale: Continued to fall (DRAM -7.94%, GLW -10.81%, AMKR -12.93%) even as the Korea source of stress rebounded sharply. Wait for Monday's full session before reassessing.
Curve little changed (10Y 4.485%). SOFR steady near IORB. First H.4.1 print in over a week is net liquidity-positive.

trade_table.signal: Watch closely — gate closest to opening yet
trade_table.rationale: Still 0/3, but KOSPI is now only ~1.4% below its 8,200 threshold and USDKRW is sitting right on 1,530. EWY itself still lags, below its SMA20.
CACC +0.21%, COF +0.15% turned positive again; only ALLY still soft (-1.65%). HY OAS steady at 2.75%.
DXY roughly flat (+0.03%) after several days of weakness. USDJPY back near 161.4, still below 162.
HYG +0.15%, OAS steady at 2.75%. Credit itself continues to show no stress despite the semis volatility.
Jul–Sep reserve drain still ahead structurally, though today's H.4.1 print (TGA drawdown, reserves up) is a near-term offset.

catalysts.note: US markets closed. Korea and other Asia markets trading their own sessions.
Next scheduled SOFR update. Full-liquidity US session resumes Monday Jul 6 — watch whether Friday's Korea rebound and US semis divergence persists.
Bid-to-cover and tail will show whether private demand absorbs supply at these higher yields.
PCE peak narrative confirmed or reversed. Hot CPI = duration selloff + Warsh hike.
APH $165/$170 spread (APH $164.59) is right at the lower strike — reassess Monday. DRAM $69/$74 and EOSE $7.50/$10 both still deep OTM.
Quarterly Refunding Announcement. Auction sizes + TGA guidance for Q3.
